name: Broadcast & OTT Content Operations Leaders — UK/AUS/UAE
angle: Post-production efficiency pain — AXIS time savings
why: Broadcast Production Managers and Heads of Content at mid-size sports broadcasters and OTT platforms own the post-production bottleneck that AXIS directly solves. Table Tennis England's 250-hour saving and 3x upload increase is a highly credible proof point for this persona. This segment is not currently targeted by any detectable active campaign.
size: Estimated 18,000–35,000 reachable on LinkedIn (UK + AUS + UAE combined)
geo: United Kingdom, Australia, United Arab Emirates
headline: 250+ hours saved in post-production. 3× more content published. Same team.
body:
Table Tennis England cut 250+ post-production hours and tripled their weekly video output in 3 months — without adding headcount.

AXIS automatically tags every clip: sport, athlete, action, sponsor brand — 100% accuracy. Your archive becomes instantly searchable. Your team stops logging and starts creating.

If you're still spending hours manually tagging and finding footage, you're leaving content (and revenue) on the table.

→ See a 20-minute AXIS walkthrough.
reason: Directly addresses post-production cost pain with a sport-specific proof point. Document Ad format recommended — stat-forward carousel will outperform image ads for this analytical persona. Bid CPL target: $160–$200.

name: League & Federation Digital Directors — Football, Hockey, Basketball — Global
angle: Fan growth and real-time highlights — PULSE matchday ROI
why: Heads of Digital and Content Directors at national federations and semi-professional leagues are responsible for social growth KPIs but lack the production capacity to publish highlights at the speed fans expect. HockeyOne (9x engagement growth) and NSL (51.8M views, 2x Instagram growth) are directly comparable organisations. This audience will self-identify with the pain immediately.
geo: United Kingdom, Australia, Germany, Netherlands, South Africa, UAE, United States
headline: 9x fan engagement growth in 7 weeks. HockeyOne ran PULSE. Here's what happened.
body:
HockeyOne 2025: 9,500+ AI clips published within seconds of the action. 10.6M video views. 264% Facebook reach increase. 9x growth in engagement (18,589 → 170,154 likes).

Previous season? Manual highlights. Standard results.

PULSE generates AI highlight clips across 15+ sports — automatically, in real-time, published directly to your social channels before fans leave the stadium.

No edit queue. No overnight turnaround. Just clips, at the speed of sport.

→ See PULSE in a 20-minute demo.
reason: Highest-credibility audience-product fit in the Spectatr portfolio. HockeyOne and NSL case studies will generate strong resonance with this persona. Single Image Ad with bold stat headline recommended for cold traffic; retarget with Document Ad carousel of full case study stats.

name: Sports Sponsorship & Partnership Directors — FMCG, Financial Services, Telco — Europe & ANZ
angle: Sponsor ROI accountability — BRAND GAUGE media value measurement
why: VP-Sponsorship and Head of Brand Partnerships roles at companies with active sports sponsorship budgets face increasing internal pressure to prove ROI against rising spend. BRAND GAUGE directly resolves this with automated, real-time media value measurement. This persona is entirely untargeted in current detectable campaigns and represents a high-value, high-intent B2B segment.
geo: United Kingdom, Germany, France, Netherlands, Australia, New Zealand
headline: Your CFO wants proof on $500K of sports sponsorship. What do you show them?
body:
Sports sponsorship budgets are at record highs — and so is the pressure to prove they worked.

BRAND GAUGE measures your actual media value in real-time: logo exposure seconds, broadcast airtime, social reach, equivalent advertising value — all automated, all auditable, all delivered without chasing rights holders for reports.

Stop estimating ROI. Start proving it — in the boardroom, at renewal, and in every campaign review.

→ Get your free sponsorship ROI report.
reason: Untested, high-intent segment with a clear pain-to-product match. Lead Gen Form recommended (lower friction for busy exec audience). CFO accountability angle differentiates from generic 'sports analytics' messaging. Suggested bid: CPL target $200–$280 reflecting seniority of audience.
